## Step 1: Learning the Pareto-Optimal Policy Set {.nav-proposed}

Learn *Pareto-optimal* Policy Family $\mathcal{F}$ via MORL.

<div style="display: flex; gap: 1em;">
<div style="flex: 1;">
::: {.callout-tip title="Definition: Policy Family"}
A policy family $\mathcal{F}: \Delta^{M-1} \rightarrow \Theta_\pi$ optimally trades off $M$ conflicting objectives $\phi: \mathcal{T} \rightarrow \mathbb{R}^M$.
:::

Each $w \in \Delta^{M-1}$ represents a tradeoff of objectives and specifies the reward $R_w(\tau) = w^T\phi(\tau)$.

Lastly, $\pi_w \in \Theta_\pi$ optimizes $R_w$.
</div>
<div style="flex: 1;">
::: {.callout-tip title="Definition: Pareto-Optimality"}
A policy $\pi_w \in \mathcal{F}$ is Pareto-optimal if no other policy improves on one objective without worsening another. $\mathcal{F}$ is the Pareto Front.

<div style="text-align: center;">
![](images/MORL.png){width="100%"}
</div>
<div style="text-align: left;">
[@moplayground]{style="font-size: 0.6em;"}
</div>
:::
</div>
</div>
